channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: ⛽ US Gasoline Market Faces Fresh Test After Near-Record Inventory Drawdowns
item.description: A near-record run of US gasoline inventory drawdowns signals fuel markets are losing their buffer just as seasonal demand peaks, raising concerns about supply constraints.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin Hosts Fifth Wartime Economic Conference as Russia Struggles for Growth Strategy
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin convenes his fifth wartime economic summit in St. Petersburg, grappling with a growth strategy as Ukrainian drone attacks strain the economy.

item.title: 🌍 US to Drastically Slash African Embassy Visa Processing
item.description: The State Department plans to cut by more than half the number of US embassies and consulates in Africa that can process visas, significantly reducing access for African travelers.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea Surpasses India as Worlds Sixth-Largest Stock Market
item.description: South Koreas equity market has overtaken Indias as the worlds sixth largest, driven by a surge in chip stocks amid global AI demand.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇮🇷 Israel and Iran Step Back From Threats After Day of Tension
item.description: President Trump announced Israel and Hezbollah agreed not to attack each other, though Netanyahu later distanced himself from cease-fire talks in Lebanon.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Agrees to Halt Attacks on Israel, Lebanon Says
item.description: Lebanon announces Hezbollah has accepted a US-backed reciprocal ceasefire with Israel. Israeli PM Netanyahu warns strikes on Beirut will continue if Hezbollah violates the agreement.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungarian PM Threatens to Oust President in Constitutional Clash
item.description: Hungary opposition leader Péter Magyar demands President Tamás Sulyok step aside, setting up a constitutional confrontation after months of political tension following the Orbán era.

item.title: 🇩🇰 Denmark Forms Centre-Left Coalition Amid Greenland Tensions with US
item.description: Mette Frederiksen secures third consecutive term as prime minister with a centre-left coalition, facing ongoing diplomatic crisis with the United States over Greenland.

item.title: 🇻🇪 Venezuelan Ruling Party Unity Fractures as Delcy Rodríguez Shifts Policy
item.description: Chavismo coalition shows cracks as Delcy Rodríguez moves away from Chávez-era policies amid Venezuela complex political and economic crisis.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Seeks Limited Interim Deal With US to Ease Economic Pressure
item.description: Iran is pushing for a limited interim agreement with the United States to ease mounting economic strain while avoiding major nuclear concessions, according to sources and analysts.

item.title: ⚔️ Iran Blames US Contradictions and Israeli Strikes for Diplomatic Delays
item.description: Iran says the slow pace of diplomacy to end the US-Iran conflict is due to a lack of trust, contradictory US positions, and continued Israeli attacks in Lebanon.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Steps Up Attacks on Russian Energy Infrastructure
item.description: Kyiv has intensified attacks on Russian energy facilities as peace talks have failed to produce progress, targeting refineries and oil infrastructure across Russia.

item.title: 🇦🇲 Russia Increases Pressure on Armenia Ahead of Election
item.description: Russia is ramping up pressure on Armenia ahead of a June 7 election as the former close ally pursues deeper ties with Brussels and Washington.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea Exports Surge to Four-Decade High on AI Chip Boom
item.description: South Korea's exports grew at the strongest annual rate in over 40 years in May as a global AI investment boom drove chip sales to record levels.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Explosions Rock Kyiv as Russia Launches Large-Scale Aerial Attack
item.description: Russia launched a large-scale aerial attack against Ukraine overnight on June 2, targeting Kyiv and cities across the country with missiles and drones amid intelligence warnings of a mass attack.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Aiming to Advance Ukraine Membership Faster Than Planned
item.description: The European Union is working to accelerate Ukraine's membership process, moving slightly faster than originally scheduled as the country continues its path toward EU integration during wartime.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Drafts Comprehensive Sanctions List Targeting US and Allies on 63 Tech Sectors
item.description: Chinese scientists have drafted a sanctions list targeting the US and its allies across 63 technology sectors, identifying sensitive or globally competitive tech that may face export restrictions.

item.title: 🛢️ Guyana Emerges as Oil Winner Amid Iran War and Strait of Hormuz Tensions
item.description: Guyana is benefiting from higher oil prices and stronger demand for non-Middle Eastern crude as the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz tensions disrupt traditional supply routes.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇷🇺🛳️ France Intercepts Second Russian Shadow Fleet Oil Tanker
item.description: The French navy boarded a sanctioned vessel linked to Russian oil trade in the Atlantic, with President Macron announcing the operation backed by UK support. Moscow condemned the action as international piracy.

item.title: 🇮🇷🛢️ Iran Suspends US Message Exchanges, Threatens Strait of Hormuz Closure
item.description: Tehran halted indirect negotiations with Washington over Israeli operations in Lebanon, with state media warning Iran may block the critical oil shipping chokepoint.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Says Iran "Really Wants to Make a Deal" as Diplomacy Continues
item.description: President Trump expressed optimism about ongoing negotiations with Tehran, stating a deal would benefit Washington and its allies amid the three-month-old conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🛢️💰 US Crude Exports Hit Record 5.6 Million Barrels Per Day
item.description: American oil exports reached unprecedented levels in May as Middle East supply disruptions from the Iran war drove demand from Asian and European refiners.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Zelenskiy Chief of Staff Sees Realistic Chance for Peace Deal by Winter
item.description: Ukrainian presidential chief of staff Kyrylo Budanov expressed optimism about reaching a peace agreement with Russia by winter, as Ukrainian drone attacks continue to disrupt Russian logistics.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇪🇺🚗 Chinas SAIC to Build First EU Car Plant in Spains Galicia
item.description: Chinese automaker SAIC Motor plans to establish its first production facility in the European Union in northwestern Spain, with an initial investment of approximately €200 million. The plant in Galicia aims to create around 1,000 direct jobs and become operational by 2028.

item.title: 🇸🇳🏛️ Senegals Ousted PM Sonko Says Party Wont Join New Government
item.description: Ousmane Sonko, Senegals recently ousted prime minister, announced his Pastef party will not participate in the new government after meeting with President Faye. The political crisis follows months of tensions amid economic challenges and an IMF program freeze over debt misreporting.
